latest.title: Buriganga river carries a city on its back
latest.script_key: look at those black streaks that s the city s runoff swirling downstream this river s so polluted it s literally eating the concrete banks it flows past that s why the waterfront keeps shrinking not growing did you know cities can collapse their own rivers this fast

latest.title: Sheep recognize faces across the flock
latest.script_key: sheep can remember familiar faces watch the face and spacing because sheep can learn who is familiar in the flock that recognition helps them stay near safe partners and avoid stress the group is not just a blur to them which farm animal should we compare next

latest.title: Cats use grooming to reset their scent map
latest.script_key: cats use grooming to reset their scent map watch the tongue and paw movements because grooming spreads familiar scent across the coat that can help a cat settle after a tense moment and keep the fur ready for the next move the cleanup is also a signal which pet behavior should we decode next

latest.title: Storm clouds reveal a storm's heat engine
latest.script_key: storm clouds show where a storm is feeding watch the spiral shape because warm ocean air rises near the center and releases heat as clouds build rotation organizes that energy into bands from above the storm is not random it is an engine which satellite clue next

latest.title: Monkeys wash food when grit gets annoying
latest.script_key: monkeys can change habits to solve small problems watch the hands near the food because some monkeys learn useful routines from their group washing or handling food differently can remove grit and make eating easier the habit spreads because it works which primate trick should come next

latest.title: Rock layers store ancient environments in stripes
latest.script_key: rock layers are time stamps made of stone watch the stripe pattern because each layer can mark a different setting river mud ocean floor windblown sand or volcanic ash stack enough layers and the cliff becomes a timeline which rock clue should we read next

latest.title: lava builds land faster than you think
latest.script_key: this lava island grew overnight because the rock melts and spreads like batter on a pan watch that crust crack open it s still stretching while it cools that s why the land pushes out faster than a city block in a decade would you step on fresh lava

latest.title: Mushrooms trade food underground in secret
latest.script_key: these mushrooms swap food underground through threads called mycelium you see those pale strands that s the network they share nutrients between trees that s why forests stay alive the big surprise it s not random they pick favorites did you learn this wrong too

latest.title: The moon keeps one face turned toward Earth
latest.script_key: the moon shows us one face for a reason watch the moon s position because its rotation and orbit are locked together it spins once in about the same time it circles earth so the same side keeps facing us that is synchronized motion not stillness which orbit clue next

latest.title: Snakes smell the air with their tongues
latest.script_key: snakes sample the air with a tongue flick watch the tongue flick because it collects scent particles and sends them to a special organ in the mouth that helps snakes track prey mates and danger without needing a loud chase the air is information would you notice the trail

latest.title: Rivers sort mud and sand along each bank
latest.script_key: rivers sort sediment while they bend watch the edge of the flow because slower water drops heavier material while faster water keeps cutting that sorting is how a quiet bend slowly redraws the map which river edge should we compare next

latest.title: ocean plastic cleanup one weird trick
latest.script_key: here s the part nobody tells you that bottle doesn t need to float forever one human step turns it from trash into trash see how the ribs bend and the plastic caves in that s why because your weight cracks the bottle s walls next time you see one step on it crunch and it s done

latest.title: Monkeys use play to survive in the jungle
latest.script_key: monkeys play in trees because it teaches them to save their own lives later watch their moves they re practicing jumps that will dodge a snake or leap away from a leopard that s why they look so silly they re training not just goofing around did your school teach you this

latest.title: Whales send songs across huge ocean distances
latest.script_key: whales can send sound farther than sight watch the body rise and listen to the idea behind it because whale sound can travel through water far better than light songs and calls help with contact over huge distances the ocean is a sound network which call should come next

latest.title: Weather stations don’t measure the forest like you think
latest.script_key: they park weather stations in trees thinking it s accurate that s why the numbers are off the canopy blocks the real wind traps heat and hides the coldest pockets below watch this one twice because the moss right there it s 5 degrees warmer than the official reading did you learn this wrong too

latest.title: Rock layers store ancient environments in stripes
latest.script_key: rock layers are time stamps made of stone watch the stripe pattern because each layer can mark a different setting river mud ocean floor windblown sand or volcanic ash stack enough layers and the cliff becomes a timeline which rock clue should we read next

latest.title: Mountains grow like living skin
latest.script_key: mountains grow from below watch the snow line peel back like sunburnt skin that s why the peaks look younger than the valleys ice and wind eat the top but stuff keeps rising from below did the textbook get this wrong too

latest.title: Bare trees survive storms better than you think
latest.script_key: this tree s bark lies to you it s not smooth for looks those cracks let wind slip through like fingers through a comb that s why it doesn t snap in storms watch the branches twist the trunk bends just enough to stay put did you learn this wrong too

latest.title: Lightning turns air into a shock wave
latest.script_key: lightning turns air into a shock wave watch the flash first because lightning heats a narrow path of air extremely fast that air expands suddenly and the pressure wave reaches us as thunder the sound is the atmosphere snapping back which storm signal next

latest.title: Forests can cool the air below them
latest.script_key: forests can cool the air below them watch the shade and mist because leaves block direct sun and release water vapor that combination can make the forest floor feel like a different climate the canopy is a cooling machine not just cover which forest layer next

latest.script_key: cats use grooming to reset their scent map watch the tongue and paw movements because grooming spreads familiar scent across the coat that can help a cat settle after a tense moment and keep the fur ready for the next move the cleanup is also a signal which pet behavior should we decode next

latest.title: Dogs read human pointing surprisingly well
latest.script_key: dogs can follow a human point fast watch the eyes check the person because dogs are unusually good at using human gestures that skill helps them cooperate with us before any command gets repeated the small glance is social intelligence not random obedience what dog skill should come next

latest.title: Plants speed growth under protected glass
latest.script_key: plants grow faster when warmth stays trapped watch the protected leaves because greenhouse glass keeps heat and humidity more stable around the plant that calmer microclimate lets growth spend less energy on stress which growing trick should we test next

latest.title: Beetles can steer by the Milky Way
latest.script_key: beetles can use the night sky like a compass watch the straight path because some dung beetles use sky patterns to keep from circling back into rivals that celestial shortcut helps a tiny insect roll food away fast would you expect a beetle to use astronomy

latest.title: Rivers carve bends by stealing from one bank
latest.script_key: rivers move sideways while they flow watch the outside of the bend because faster water cuts that bank while slower water drops sediment inside the curve over time the whole river shifts across the landscape a bend is motion written in mud which water shape next
